title: Palakkavu Bhagavathi temple
tagline: Palakkavu Bhagavathi temple — spiritual town of Kerala

overview.short: The Edava Palakkavu Bhagavathi temple (ഇടവാ പാലക്കാവ് ഭഗവതി ക്ഷേത്രം) is one of the ancient temples in south India.
overview.description: The Edava Palakkavu Bhagavathi temple (ഇടവാ പാലക്കാവ് ഭഗവതി ക്ഷേത്രം) is one of the ancient temples in south India. It is situated at Edava in Thiruvananthapuram District, India. The temple is situated 5.7km North of Varkala City.Bhadrakali Devi is the presiding deity of this temple. A festival is conducted during the month of "Kumbham".

topPlaces.name: Edava
topPlaces.description: Edava is a panchayat in Varkala Taluk, and lies at the northernmost tip of Thiruvananthapuram district in the state of Kerala, India. Edava is located 5 km north of Varkala, 21 km south of port city Kollam, and 44 km north of capital city Thiruvananthapuram.

topPlaces.name: Kappil, Thiruvananthapuram
topPlaces.description: Kappil is a seashore tourist spot located in thiruvananthapuram district, Kerala, India. Paravoor railway station is the major railway station near Kappil.

topPlaces.name: Varkala
topPlaces.description: Varkala, historically known as Balita is a major coastal municipality and a northern suburb of Thiruvananthapuram, the capital city of the Indian state of Kerala. In legends and myths related to Kerala, texts like the Kerala Mahatmyam describe the Varkala region as one of the sacred places of Kerala.

topPlaces.name: Kurakkanni
topPlaces.description: Kurakkanni is the north western area of Varkala Town in Varkala Municipality of Trivandrum district in the state of Kerala, India. It is one of the fastest growing commercial-residential area of Varkala town situated 3.6 km (2.2 mi) from the town centre, 25 km (16 mi) south of Kollam City, 43 km (27 mi) north of Trivandrum City along Trivandrum - Varkala - Kollam coastal highway road.

topPlaces.name: Janardanaswamy Temple
topPlaces.description: The Janardanaswamy Temple, also known as the Varkala Temple, is a Hindu temple situated in Janardhanapuram, Varkala city, Thiruvananthapuram district, Kerala, India. The temple is dedicated to the worship of deity Vishnu in the form of Janardanaswamy, locally known as Varkaleswara (the lord of Varkala).

topPlaces.name: Thekkumbhagam
topPlaces.description: Thekkumbhagam or Thekkumbhagom is the western coastal town of Paravur municipality in the Kollam district of Kerala, India. It is located at the South-western tip of Kollam's coastal area.

topPlaces.name: Kottappuram, Paravur
topPlaces.description: Kottappuram or Kottapuram is an urban village, situated at Paravur municipality in Kollam district, Kerala. It is one among the two villages in Paravur municipality and one among the 30 villages coming under Kollam Taluk.

topPlaces.name: Varkala Taluk
topPlaces.description: Varkala Taluk is a Taluk (tehsil) situated in the northernmost tip of Thiruvananthapuram district in the Indian state of Kerala. It is the 3rd most densely populated taluk in the district comprising 10 villages and Varkala municipality.

items.note: Edava is a panchayat in Varkala Taluk, and lies at the northernmost tip of Thiruvananthapuram district in the state of Kerala, India. Edava is located 5 km north of Varkala, 21 km south of port city Kollam, and 44 km north of capital city Thiruvananthapuram.
Kappil is a seashore tourist spot located in thiruvananthapuram district, Kerala, India. Paravoor railway station is the major railway station near Kappil.

Varkala, historically known as Balita is a major coastal municipality and a northern suburb of Thiruvananthapuram, the capital city of the Indian state of Kerala. In legends and myths related to Kerala, texts like the Kerala Mahatmyam describe the Varkala region as one of the sacred places of Kerala.

Kurakkanni is the north western area of Varkala Town in Varkala Municipality of Trivandrum district in the state of Kerala, India. It is one of the fastest growing commercial-residential area of Varkala town situated 3.6 km (2.2 mi) from the town centre, 25 km (16 mi) south of Kollam City, 43 km (27 mi) north of Trivandrum City along Trivandrum - Varkala - Kollam coastal highway road.

The Janardanaswamy Temple, also known as the Varkala Temple, is a Hindu temple situated in Janardhanapuram, Varkala city, Thiruvananthapuram district, Kerala, India. The temple is dedicated to the worship of deity Vishnu in the form of Janardanaswamy, locally known as Varkaleswara (the lord of Varkala).
Thekkumbhagam or Thekkumbhagom is the western coastal town of Paravur municipality in the Kollam district of Kerala, India. It is located at the South-western tip of Kollam's coastal area.

Kottappuram or Kottapuram is an urban village, situated at Paravur municipality in Kollam district, Kerala. It is one among the two villages in Paravur municipality and one among the 30 villages coming under Kollam Taluk.

Varkala Taluk is a Taluk (tehsil) situated in the northernmost tip of Thiruvananthapuram district in the Indian state of Kerala. It is the 3rd most densely populated taluk in the district comprising 10 villages and Varkala municipality.

faq.q: What is the best time to visit Palakkavu Bhagavathi temple?
faq.a: The best time to visit Palakkavu Bhagavathi temple is Nov – Feb. Summers are 26–36°C and winters are 22–32°C.

faq.q: How do I reach Palakkavu Bhagavathi temple?
faq.a: The nearest airport is Thiruvananthapuram International Airport (40 km) and the nearest railway station is Thiruvananthapuram Central (42 km). Roads are generally good; check seasonal/monsoon conditions before high-altitude or remote stretches.

faq.q: How many days are enough for Palakkavu Bhagavathi temple?
faq.a: 3 days are usually enough to cover the top attractions of Palakkavu Bhagavathi temple at a relaxed pace.

faq.q: How much does a hotel cost in Palakkavu Bhagavathi temple?
faq.a: Stays in Palakkavu Bhagavathi temple start from ₹900 per night, with options across 4 price categories.

faq.q: What are the top places to visit in Palakkavu Bhagavathi temple?
faq.a: The top attractions are Edava, Kappil, Thiruvananthapuram, Varkala, Kurakkanni, Janardanaswamy Temple.
